results.possibleIdentity: Wayne Bennett was a technical participant and member of the Toronto chapter of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) during the late 1960s and early 1970s. At the time of his association with E.A.T. (circa 1968–1969), he was a student of civil engineering in Toronto, Ontario. He registered with the organization's Technical Services program, which facilitated collaborations between the artistic and technical communities. In his later career, he is recognized as an artist, researcher, and illustrator who became a long-term collaborator with the landscape archaeologist Christopher Tilley. Together, they co-authored several significant works in the field of landscape phenomenology, including "The Materiality of Stone: Explorations in Landscape Phenomenology" (2004) and "Body and Image: Explorations in Landscape Phenomenology 2" (2008). His work often involved the technical documentation, drawing, and phenomenological mapping of archaeological sites and landscapes, bridging his early technical background in civil engineering with artistic and archaeological research.
